title: Don't Look Up
logline: Two astronomers discover a comet heading toward Earth and struggle to warn humanity through a media and political landscape more interested in ratings and profits than survival.
protagonist: Dr. Randall Mindy and Kate Dibiasky
antagonistOrConflict: Systemic indifference, media manipulation, and corporate greed in the face of existential crisis

actBreaks.description: The scientists leave the White House after being dismissed, marking the end of their initial attempt to warn authorities and the beginning of their media campaign.

validation.ruling: Scene #28 introduces Peter Isherwell and BASH corporation at a tech conference, fundamentally shifting the story from a pure disaster narrative to one about corporate interference, establishing the human antagonist who will later derail the mission.

actBreaks.description: The Bubba Gump's restaurant incident leads to the scientists being arrested and discredited, ending their public influence and beginning the final countdown to impact.

validation.ruling: Scene #110 shows the complete breakdown of the scientific team - Kate is arrested and hooded, Teddy abandons Randall, and Bubba Gump's burns as an angry mob riots, representing the irreversible collapse of rational discourse and the point of no return for humanity.

naturalStructure.structuralPersonality: This script follows a classic disaster movie structure but inverts it through satirical lens. Rather than building toward heroic triumph, it chronicles systematic failure at every institutional level. The first act establishes the threat and initial bureaucratic indifference. The second act becomes a media circus where the message gets corrupted by celebrity culture and political spin. The third act abandons all pretense of salvation, focusing instead on human connection amid inevitable doom. The structure mirrors the comet's trajectory - starting small and distant, gaining momentum and visibility, until it dominates everything.
naturalStructure.pacingNotes: The pacing accelerates dramatically after scene 69 when the mission launches, then maintains breakneck speed through rapid-fire montages and cross-cutting between locations. The final act slows to intimate moments between characters while simultaneously showing global chaos, creating emotional whiplash that serves the satirical purpose.

incitingIncident.description: Kate estimates the object is 135 to 150 kilometers wide while the grad students express disbelief at the massive size.
incitingIncident.sceneHeading: INT. MAUNA KEA KECK TWO TELESCOPE - SAME TIME

validation.ruling: Scene #5 is too early in the discovery process - it's just confirmation calls between scientists. Scene #6 is the true inciting incident where Kate reveals the asteroid is 135-150 kilometers wide, making the characters realize this isn't just any space rock but a planet-killer that shatters their ordinary world and establishes the main conflict.

midpoint.description: Colonel Drask shakes in the shuttle cockpit six minutes from booster dump, making racist and homophobic comments to everyone on Earth before Mission Control cuts him off.
midpoint.sceneHeading: INT. SHUTTLE COCKPIT - THE UPPER ATMOSPHERE - SAME TIME

validation.ruling: Scene #99 shows a racist astronaut's radio chatter, which isn't a structural turning point. Scene #101 shows the mission appearing successful in the Situation Room with Randall and Brie kissing, representing a false victory moment before everything changes - this is the calm before Isherwell's intervention derails the plan.

climax.description: As the house shakes violently and they discuss coffee, the asteroid impacts Earth, splitting the planet apart in a massive explosion of debris.
climax.sceneHeading: INT. MINDY'S DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - CONTINUOUS

validation.ruling: Scene #164 shows the asteroid entering atmosphere, but that's setup, not the climax itself. Scene #167 delivers the actual climax where the asteroid impacts Earth, splitting the planet apart in a massive explosion - this is the ultimate confrontation with the antagonist (the asteroid) and resolves the main story question with maximum dramatic tension.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MAUNA KEA TELESCOPE ARRAY - HAWAII - DUSK
scenes.summary: The famous telescopes of Mauna Kea sit on a mountain framed by an orange sunset.

scenes.heading: INT. KECK OBSERVATORY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Professor Randall Mindy tells pulsar jokes to his grad students when PhD candidate Kate Dibiasky enters the observatory.

scenes.heading: INT. KECK TWO OBSERVATORY - TWENTY MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: The grad students celebrate Kate's comet discovery with beer and snacks while Randall calculates the 6.6 billion to one odds of witnessing a comet Near-Earth Object collision.

scenes.heading: INT. CAL-TECH, PASADENA - HALLWAY - MINUTES LATER - DUSK
scenes.summary: Dr. Calder walks quickly down a hallway at Cal-Tech, clearly having other plans interrupted.

scenes.heading: INT. CENTER FOR NEO STUDIES - OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Calder takes a phone call from Randall about a Near-Earth Object that changed orbit after interacting with a comet, and begins checking their automated detection systems.
scenes.turningPointNote: First official scientific confirmation that something significant has happened with the asteroid

scenes.heading: INT. MAUNA KEA KECK TWO TELESCOPE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate estimates the object is 135 to 150 kilometers wide while the grad students express disbelief at the massive size.

scenes.heading: EXT. GOVERNMENT OFFICE BUILDINGS, WASHINGTON DC - DAY
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of bland government office buildings in Washington DC housing the Planetary Defense Coordination Office.

scenes.heading: INT. OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Oglethorpe answers his phone and mentions that Cambridge got a hit but assumed it was a glitch, and he's received emails from Norway.

scenes.heading: INT. CAL-TECH, PASADENA - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Calder reads printouts showing initial calculations of a 145 kilometer wide asteroid with a trajectory bringing it very close to Earth.

scenes.heading: INT. MAUNA KEA KECK TWO TELESCOPE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate and the grad students frantically hand-check calculations while Randall speaks to Dr. Oglethorpe, with students calculating near misses ranging from 31,000 to 82,000 kilometers in about 5 months.

scenes.heading: EXT. HICKAM AIR FORCE BASE - HONOLULU - 30 MINUTES LATER -
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall are rushed onto a massive C-5 Galaxy transport plane at Hickam Air Force Base by a Captain shouting orders.

scenes.heading: INT. C-5 GALAXY PLANE - HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall sit alone in the vast empty cargo hold of the military transport plane during their flight.

scenes.heading: EXT. JOINT BASE ANACOSTIA BOLLING - 7 HOURS LATER - 11 AM
scenes.summary: Marines briefly put black sacks over Kate and Randall's heads before a Sergeant corrects the mistake and directs them to a town car.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE - 30 MINUTES LATER - DAY
scenes.summary: Tourists and souvenir stands are visible outside the White House.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall and Kate meet Dr. Oglethorpe and General Themes in the White House hallway, with Kate pointing out the nearby Oval Office.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - A FEW HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: The President walks past the waiting group without acknowledging them, and they wait for nine hours while dealing with a Supreme Court nominee crisis, never getting called into the meeting.

scenes.heading: EXT. RED ROOF INN - WASHINGTON, DC - LATER THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of a Red Roof Inn on a cold, slushy night with heavy traffic.

scenes.heading: INT. KATE’S ROOM - WASHINGTON, DC - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate tells her boyfriend Phillip over the phone that they waited nine hours and were never called in to meet the President.

scenes.heading: INT. WEBSITE OFFICES - MANHATTAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Phillip explains from his news website office that President Orlean is in crisis over her Supreme Court nominee's background as a nude model and controversial sheriff record.

scenes.heading: INT. RANDALL’S ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall calls his wife from his motel room while entertainment news about Riley Bina and DJ Chello's breakup plays on TV.

scenes.heading: INT. MIDDLE CLASS HOME - LANSING, MICHIGAN - DAY
scenes.summary: June encourages Randall to speak up in tomorrow's meeting and take credit for his brilliance, while their three sons are visible in the background.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE - WASHINGTON DC - THE NEXT AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of the White House exterior the next afternoon.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randall explains the asteroid collision and trajectory to President Orlean, who dismisses the extinction-level threat and orders them to sit tight until after midterm elections, threatening imprisonment if they leak the information.
scenes.turningPointNote: The President's shocking decision to suppress the asteroid threat for political reasons creates the central conflict

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE HALLWAY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Dr. Oglethorpe warns Randall and Kate not to speak until they're clear of the White House, while Kate questions the President's son's entertainment background.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUBBA GUMP'S SHRIMP - AN HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of Bubba Gump's Shrimp restaurant exterior.

scenes.heading: INT. BUBBA GUMP’S SHRIMP - WASHINGTON DC - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: At Bubba Gump's, the trio discusses leaking the story since waiting three weeks means the asteroid requires more force to divert, with Randall revealing his panic attacks.
scenes.turningPointNote: The decision to leak the story to the press represents a major plot turn against government orders

scenes.heading: INT. WASHINGTON UNION STATION - 40 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Kate, Randall, and Teddy rush through Union Station to catch a train, with Kate arranging to meet reporter Adul Grelio through her boyfriend.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT CONVENTION CENTER - SAN FRANCISCO - DAY
scenes.summary: A massive tech presentation unfolds with Philip Glass-style music and imagery of human connection, featuring a birth scene transitioning through a cell phone to an aircraft carrier.

scenes.heading: INT. BACK STAGE - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Tech CEO Peter Isherwell angrily criticizes his presentation's abstract tagline while dismissing a journalist asking about manufacturing costs.

scenes.heading: INT. BALTHAZAR RESTAURANT - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall show journalist Adul Grelio pictures of the comet-asteroid collision at Balthazar restaurant, with Kate providing a zip drive of mathematical proof.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK HERALD OFFICE - 30 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: At the New York Herald, editors and owners discuss the asteroid story's confirmation, but their phones buzz with news that BASH Cellular has bought the newspaper.
scenes.turningPointNote: The corporate buyout of the newspaper threatens to kill the asteroid story

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW YORK CITY STREET - 20 MINUTES LATER - LATE AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: Kate reveals to Phillip and a passerby that an asteroid three times larger than the one that killed dinosaurs is headed for Earth, then claims it's for a video game.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Something massive and monstrous moves purposefully forward through total blackness in space.

scenes.heading: INT. POSH OFFICE IN SOHO - TWO HOURS LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Media coach Sam Rand trains Kate and Randall for television appearances, dressing them stylishly while coaching them to soften their urgent message about the asteroid.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY'S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: June Mindy tells Randall over the phone that this is why he's spent his life in science and that the world needs him.

scenes.heading: INT. POSH SOHO OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Randall gets emotional talking to his wife while Sam's team coaches Kate to deliver a watered-down message that downplays the asteroid's danger.

scenes.heading: INT. PHILLIP’S APARTMENT - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kate and Phillip eat Indian food while watching news about the Supreme Court nominee's porn scandal, and Phillip mentions his website was bought by BASH Cellular.

scenes.heading: EXT. MIDTOWN MANHATTAN - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: Crowds wait outside The Daily Rip studio in midtown Manhattan.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall get their hair and makeup done while Sam Rand coaches them that television is just having a conversation, and they review their planned talking points.

scenes.heading: INT. LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: June Mindy and Randall's three sons eat breakfast while waiting for The Daily Rip to start, with June encouraging him to breathe and speak up.

scenes.heading: INT. CRAPPY APARTMENT - WASHINGTON DC - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Teddy drinks coffee in his sparsely decorated apartment while watching TV, surrounded by pictures of his kids and one Eagles player photo.

scenes.heading: INT. AUTOPSY OFFICES - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Phillip eats a Danish while watching TV in the packed Autopsy office.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK HERALD OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Herald's Chief Editor hangs up a call and tells Adul Grelio that the White House is denying they met with Kate and Randall, classifying the asteroid as a near miss.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Daily Rip hosts Jack Bremmer and Brie Evantee joke about erasing 80s pornos and discuss Sheriff Conlon's continued party support despite the scandal.

scenes.heading: INT. GREEN ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate and Randall wait in the green room with actress Riley Bina, while a stage manager incorrectly tells them they'll discuss the planet they discovered.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - 30 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Riley Bina tells the Daily Rip hosts she still loves DJ Chello and wants to take him back despite his cheating.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY’S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Mindy sons watch Riley Bina's interview while reporting that Twitter exploded and BASH News sent eight push notifications about the breakup.

scenes.heading: INT. TEDDY OGELTHORPE’S APARTMENT - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Teddy watches Riley Bina's interview and comments approvingly about forgiveness being the whole game.

scenes.heading: INT. AUTOPSY OFFICES - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Autopsy staff immediately holds a meeting to capitalize on Riley Bina's announcement, assigning stories about celebrity reconciliations while Phillip glances at the TV.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - 10 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Jack and Brie return from commercial break discussing the intense show, and the stage manager cues Kate and Randall to take their seats as the hosts introduce them as having made a discovery in space.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY'S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: June and their sons watch Randall on TV, with their 23-year-old son commenting that Dad looks good.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: On The Daily Rip, Randall and Kate explain their discovery of an asteroid collision that changed K-1684's orbit to hit Earth catastrophically in five months, while the hosts treat it lightly and ask trivial questions.

scenes.heading: EXT. MANHATTAN BUILDING - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Randall chases Kate down a Manhattan street as she expresses frustration that their scientific warning was treated like entertainment, while Randall insists people did hear their message.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK HERALD OFFICE - AN HOUR LATER - AFTERNOON
scenes.summary: At the New York Herald office, a media analyst shows engagement statistics revealing their TV appearance was a 'bomb engagement' with minimal pickup except in scientific circles and fringe end times sites.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE DARK SIDE OF JUPITER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The giant asteroid moves through space near Jupiter while Kate describes how it will extinct all life on Earth.

scenes.heading: INT. NEW YORK HERALD OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate reveals she put the impact moment on a diet app and is crying five times a day, then receives a BASH notification about an article by her ex-boyfriend calling her crazy.

scenes.heading: EXT. PRIVATE AIRFIELD IN SHANGHAI - NIGHT - A WEEK LATER
scenes.summary: Peter Isherwell meets with Chinese businessmen and a general at a Shanghai airfield, discussing BASH's 21% ownership of US media and seeking partnership in African mining operations.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE LAWN - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Reporter Dalia Hensfield reports from the White House lawn that President Orlean sent nude photos to Supreme Court nominee Sheriff Conlon and they've been having an affair for years.

scenes.heading: INT. DR. MINDY’S HOME - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall argues with people on Twitter about science, getting excited when his response gets three likes, until June tells him someone is there to see him.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY’S LIVING ROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall enters his living room to find seven FBI agents inside and five black sedans with flashing lights on his front lawn.

scenes.heading: EXT. MICHIGAN STATE CAMPUS - SAME DAY, SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate jogs across campus when undercover FBI agents disguised as students, a blind man, and a coffee vendor draw guns and arrest her for breach of national security secrets.

scenes.heading: EXT. “OFFICE 4 YOU” OFFICE BUILDING - D.C. - 1 HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: FBI agents in three black sedans arrest Teddy Oglethorpe outside an office building, with Teddy making nervous comments about skin pigmentation as he gets on the ground.

scenes.heading: INT. BLACK SEDAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate sits hooded in the back of an FBI sedan, cursing at the two federal agents driving her.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE - SIX HOURS LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Establishing shot of the White House at night.

scenes.heading: INT. HALLWAY OUTSIDE THE OVAL OFFICE - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Teddy, Randall, and Kate sit under FBI guard in the hallway outside the Oval Office, with Teddy speculating they're being leveraged, until Jason invites them in.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - TEN MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean officially apologizes and announces they will mobilize to save the planet, admitting they're acting now because the midterm scandal makes it politically advantageous.
scenes.turningPointNote: Government finally commits to action on the asteroid threat

scenes.heading: EXT. LINCOLN MEMORIAL - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean addresses the nation from the Lincoln Memorial, announcing the asteroid threat and the emergency mission using nuclear-loaded space shuttles.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - FLASHBACK
scenes.summary: Flashback to Jason discussing the emergency spending package for Congress in the Oval Office.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE FRONT LAWN - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean continues her speech about the emergency spending package and military mission to knock the asteroid off course.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - TWO NIGHTS PRIOR
scenes.summary: Flashback to the Oval Office where President Orlean says they need a hero pilot, with Randall questioning why remote technology isn't used.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINCOLN MEMORIAL ADDRESS - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean announces Medal of Honor recipient Colonel Benedict Drask will lead the dangerous mission.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - TWO NIGHTS EARLIER
scenes.summary: Flashback to the Oval Office where they discuss Drask's heroic military record and his unfortunate incident with children.

scenes.heading: EXT. WHITE HOUSE FRONT LAWN 3 YEARS EARLIER - DAY
scenes.summary: Flashback to Colonel Drask leading children in jumping jacks on the White House lawn and beginning to use a homophobic slur.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINCOLN MEMORIAL ADDRESS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: President Orlean quotes Drask's supposed final words about earning the extra living his sacrifice would give everyone.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: Flashback to Jason admitting he wrote Drask's line, with Kate recognizing it from Saving Private Ryan.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINCOLN MEMORIAL ADDRESS - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Colonel Drask salutes and reports for duty while President Orlean thanks the scientists, mispronouncing Kate's name.

scenes.heading: INT. OVAL OFFICE - FLASH BACK TWO NIGHTS PRIOR
scenes.summary: Flashback to Jason explaining the scientists need to be the public face of the effort, with President Orlean noting Randall's higher Q rating and Kate's appeal to disaffected youth and prisoners.

scenes.heading: EXT. LINCOLN MEMORIAL ADDRESS - 2 NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean concludes her speech promising to address the UN and work with the world unified, as social media buzzes about the asteroid.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Old space shuttles are towed onto the tarmac at Kennedy Space Center.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall appears on The Daily Rip assuring viewers the mission will work, while Brie puts her hand on his thigh and he gets visibly aroused.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPREME COURT CHAMBERS - DAY
scenes.summary: Sheriff Conlon is sworn in as the newest Supreme Court Justice.

scenes.heading: EXT. IDAHO NUCLEAR FACILITY - DAY
scenes.summary: ICBMs are transported from an Idaho nuclear facility to Kennedy Center.

scenes.heading: INT. PATRIOT NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Kate and Teddy debate Congressman Tenant on Patriot News, with the congressman dismissing them and calling Kate 'Ms. Crazy hair.'

scenes.heading: INT. AIRFORCE ONE - DAY
scenes.summary: On Air Force One, President Orlean explains she's giving Congress a 2% tax cut for the wealthy and a law requiring gay doctors to disclose their sexuality to patients in exchange for the spending bill.

scenes.heading: EXT. EAST LAWN - TWO DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean signs the bill into law on the East Lawn, calling it the bill that may save mankind while also giving tax relief and religious freedom.

scenes.heading: INT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER MEDICAL CENTER - DAY
scenes.summary: A physician informs Colonel Drask that federal mandate requires him to disclose his homosexual encounters in college, shocking the colonel.

scenes.heading: EXT. D.C. HOTEL - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Reporter Dalia Hensfield reports that President Orlean's party swept the midterms, giving her a super majority and 98% approval rating.

scenes.heading: INT. JIMMY FALLON SHOW - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Randall appears on Jimmy Fallon's show with a new clean-shaven look, joking that he turns to Fallon's show for comfort when frightened.

scenes.heading: INT. FANCY HOTEL - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall and Brie have sex in a hotel hallway while she asks him to tell her how everyone will die, as he receives a loving text from his wife June.

scenes.heading: INT. CABLE NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Kate appears on cable news where a viewer poll shows only 44% believe mankind's future is at stake, frustrating Kate when the host treats science as opinion.

scenes.heading: INT. HOTEL ROOM BEDROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Brie and Randall lie in bed post-sex as she reveals her wealthy background, multiple degrees, famous ex-husbands, and affairs with presidents, then asks about his background.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE SITUATION ROOM - DAY
scenes.summary: The team watches monitors in the White House Situation Room as Colonel Drask reports ready for launch from the Kennedy Center launch pad.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - 3 MILES AWAY - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: A hundred thousand people watch from three miles away as President Orlean thanks Colonel Drask over radio, saying Jesus Christ thanks him.

scenes.heading: EXT. TOKYO CENTER CITY - SAME TIME - NIGHT
scenes.summary: A hundred thousand people in Tokyo watch the launch on giant screens.

scenes.heading: EXT. CAIRO EYGPT - SAME TIME - DAY
scenes.summary: Hundreds of thousands watch giant screens in Cairo, Egypt.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE SITUATION ROOM - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: President Orlean gives Randall the honor of authorizing launch as Kate notices him holding hands with Brie, then Peter Isherwell suddenly enters the situation room.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The shuttles and satellites successfully lift off from Kennedy Space Center with bright flames battling gravity.

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE SITUATION ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Everyone celebrates the successful launch with no satellite malfunctions, increasing mission success probability, until Peter Isherwell pulls President Orlean aside demanding to speak privately.

scenes.heading: INT. SHUTTLE COCKPIT - THE UPPER ATMOSPHERE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Colonel Drask shakes in the shuttle cockpit six minutes from booster dump, making racist and homophobic comments to everyone on Earth before Mission Control cuts him off.

scenes.heading: EXT. HALLWAY OUTSIDE THE SITUATION ROOM - SECONDS LATER
scenes.summary: Peter Isherwell explains something to President Orlean in the hallway outside the Situation Room, cutting her off when she tries to respond.
scenes.turningPointNote: Peter Isherwell's private intervention with the President during the critical mission launch suggests a major plot shift

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE SITUATION ROOM - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: In the White House Situation Room, as shuttles approach the atmosphere, Randall and Brie kiss while watching the mission unfold.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: At Kennedy Space Center, massive crowds cheer the space shuttle launch until onlookers notice the lead shuttle is turning around instead of continuing its mission.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mission is aborted, fundamentally changing the trajectory of the asteroid response

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE OFFICE - TWO HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall, Kate, and Teddy are locked in a White House office for two hours until Jason arrives to apologize for keeping them confined.

scenes.heading: INT. CABINET ROOM - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: President Orlean announces to her full cabinet that the asteroid is actually an opportunity, with Peter Isherwell standing behind her as she reveals BASH's spectrometer readings.
scenes.turningPointNote: The government officially pivots from destroying the asteroid to mining it for profit

scenes.heading: INT. WHITE HOUSE OFFICE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate and Teddy sit stressed and half-asleep in the White House office, eating snacks and discussing a general who charged them for free food, until Randall returns.

scenes.heading: EXT. BUBBA GUMP'S SHRIMP - AN HOUR LATER - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Exterior establishing shot of Bubba Gump's Shrimp restaurant at night.

scenes.heading: INT. BUBBA GUMP'S SHRIMP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: At Bubba Gump's Shrimp, Randall explains BASH's plan to Kate and Teddy, defending the company's credible science while Kate argues he's unqualified to judge engineering.

scenes.heading: INT. CABINET ROOM - EARLIER
scenes.summary: In a flashback to the Cabinet Room, Isherwell presents an animation showing BASH drones drilling explosives into the asteroid to break it into smaller pieces.

scenes.heading: INT. BUBBA GUMP'S SHRIMP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate and Teddy challenge the BASH plan's flaws while Randall defends his role as questioner, leading Kate and Teddy to walk out when Randall refuses to oppose the government and corporation.
scenes.turningPointNote: The scientific team fractures as Kate and Teddy abandon the official response

scenes.heading: EXT. BUBBA GUMP'S SHRIMP - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Outside the restaurant, people smash windows and fight with police while Kate sits hooded in a sedan as Randall and Teddy apologize through the window.

scenes.heading: INT. PATRIOT NEWS STUDIO - NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: President Orlean tells Dan Pawketty in a TV interview that the asteroid is a miracle from God that will create jobs and harm no one.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT MILITARY TRANSPORT PLANE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Kate sits hooded in the back of a military transport plane flying to an unknown destination.

scenes.heading: INT. PATRIOT NEWS STUDIO - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: President Orlean dismisses Kate as mentally ill during her TV interview with Dan Pawketty, comparing the asteroid to manna from heaven.

scenes.heading: EXT. SCOTT AIRFORCE BASE - ST.CLAIR, ILLINOIS - MORNING
scenes.summary: At Scott Air Force Base, a federal attorney reads charges against Kate and offers to drop prosecution if she agrees to media silence and accepts transfer to a different university.

scenes.heading: EXT. ILLINOIS HIGHWAY - DAY
scenes.summary: Kate rides in the back of a cargo van along an Illinois highway.

scenes.heading: EXT. LARGE UNIVERSITY - DAY
scenes.summary: Thousands of students protest at a university, chanting against BASH cellular while Dr. Calder speaks about divesting from the company.

scenes.heading: INT. GNN CABLE NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Harrison Telms reports polling numbers showing divided public opinion on the asteroid, with 40% supporting job creation, 37% opposing impact, and 23% denying its existence.

scenes.heading: EXT. LOWER MIDDLE CLASS ILLINOIS HOME - DUSK
scenes.summary: Kate's parents refuse to let her into their home unless she agrees to no political talk, revealing they support the jobs the asteroid will provide.

scenes.heading: INT. THE WASHINGTON DC HYATT - A FEW NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall returns to his hotel suite with Brie to find his wife June waiting with luggage, leading to a confrontation where Brie refuses to feel bad about their affair.

scenes.heading: EXT. SOUTHERN ILLINOIS TOWN - FEW DAYS LATER.
scenes.summary: A Southern Illinois town displays anti-asteroid denial sentiment with bumper stickers and store signs rejecting "Never Hitters."

scenes.heading: INT. BEV-MO STORE - ILLINOIS - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Kate works as a cashier at Bev-Mo while watching TV coverage of Teddy debating a moderate congressman about asteroid wealth distribution.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - FLORIDA - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: At Kennedy Space Center, Randall confronts President Orlean and Isherwell about impact deniers and presents concerning documents about the mission.

scenes.heading: EXT. OLD BURGER KING PARKING LOT - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kate drinks with young people in a Burger King parking lot, listening to them discuss BASH's payment to Chile and rumors of executive escape plans.

scenes.heading: EXT. OUT OF THE WAY NYC RESTAURANT - TWO NIGHTS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall meets journalist Adul Grelio at a restaurant and shows him the same documents he presented to Isherwell, while radio news reports Chilean refugees heading to the US border.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The asteroid moves through space toward the small blue planet Earth, fully illuminated by sunlight.

scenes.heading: INT. THE DAILY RIP STUDIO - TWO DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: On The Daily Rip, Brie and Jack discuss a Netflix asteroid movie before interviewing a tense Randall, who appears awkward under the bright studio lights.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY’S MIDDLE CLASS HOME - LANSING - THE NEXT DAY
scenes.summary: June watches news of journalist Adul Grelio's arrest at her home, then closes the door on Randall when he arrives seeking reconciliation.

scenes.heading: EXT. ROOF OF A CLOSED BURGER KING - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: Kate and Quentin lie on a Burger King roof looking at stars, where Kate reveals her astronomy background and they spot something visible in the sky before kissing.
scenes.turningPointNote: The asteroid becomes visible to the naked eye, making denial impossible

scenes.heading: INT. CRAPPY APARTMENT - LANSING - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall receives an email revoking his tenure, then gets a call from Kate telling him to look up at the now-visible asteroid while her viral video gains millions of retweets.

scenes.heading: EXT. RED CARPET PREMIERE - NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a movie premiere, Tessa Thompson wears a "Just Look Up" pin while Vin Diesel wears a neutral pin pointing both up and down.

scenes.heading: INT. PATRIOT NEWS STUDIO - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall debates Congressman Tenant on Patriot News about the visible meteor, with the congressman deflecting by questioning Randall's authority and invoking freedom.

scenes.heading: EXT. RALLEY IN GIANT BASKETBALL STADIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: President Orlean rallies 20,000 supporters in a basketball stadium, telling them not to look up and putting on a "Don't Look Up" hat as the crowd chants.

scenes.heading: EXT. BASH STORE - NEW YORK - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Quentin and friends run away after spray painting "Just Look Up!" on a BASH cell phone store.

scenes.heading: EXT. MEXICAN BORDER - DAY
scenes.summary: News footage shows over two million Chilean migrants massed at the Mexican border, with President Orlean warning of potential "Just Look Up" terrorists.

scenes.heading: INT. GIANT OFFICE SPACE - QUEENS - DAY
scenes.summary: At Just Look Up headquarters, Randall emerges from his office with a revelation and tells Kate, Teddy, and Dr. Calder they should have called someone long ago.

scenes.heading: INT. WELL APPOINTED LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bono answers his phone in his well-appointed living room, asking how he can help.

scenes.heading: EXT. WEMBLEY STADIUM - NIGHT
scenes.summary: Bono and U2 perform at Wembley Stadium before 120,000 fans, calling for all nations to send nukes to deflect the asteroid, sparking massive worldwide protests.

scenes.heading: INT. JUST LOOK UP HEADQUARTERS - 3 DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Teddy reports that Orlean and BASH have bought off China, Russia, India, and the UK with mineral rights, leaving only South Korea refusing but lacking sufficient nuclear capability.
scenes.turningPointNote: All hope for international intervention is eliminated, sealing humanity's fate

scenes.heading: EXT. DON’T LOOK UP RALLY - GREAT LAWN, D.C. - THAT NIGHT
scenes.summary: At a Don't Look Up rally, Congressman Tenant addresses a giant crowd wearing red, white, and blue caps, attacking Just Look Uppers while one supporter briefly glances up at the sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. HIGHWAY - TWO DAYS LATER
scenes.summary: Randall, Kate, and Quentin drive along a completely empty highway toward the Midwest to go grocery shopping while everyone else watches the BASH launch.

scenes.heading: INT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: President Orlean announces the launch of Operation Claim God's Gift from Kennedy Space Center's command center, blessing everyone except South Korea.

scenes.heading: INT. SUPER MARKET - MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall, Kate, and Quentin shop for groceries including real salmon and sweet potatoes while Quentin steals a steak.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: Massive crowds watch the launch of 30 BEAD drones at Kennedy Space Center with quiet trepidation as the huge asteroid is visible in the day sky.

scenes.heading: EXT. THE MINDY'S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - DAY
scenes.summary: Randall approaches his family home with Kate and Quentin, carrying groceries, and knocks on the door.

scenes.heading: INT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: At Kennedy Space Center, BEAD drone number 12 explodes and number 22 fails to launch, with Isherwell insisting they anticipated the margin of error.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The massive crowd at Kennedy Space Center watches the drone satellites launch in total silence instead of cheering.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY'S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: June opens the door to Randall and lets him, Kate, and Quentin into the family home for dinner, with Randall sobbing as he hugs his wife.

scenes.heading: EXT. DEEP SPACE - TWO HOURS LATER
scenes.summary: The BEAD drones approach the asteroid in deep space, but two more fail to attach and explode into each other due to lateral control malfunction.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY'S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The Mindy family prepares dinner together with Kate and Quentin helping, while Teddy arrives with wine and joins the gathering.

scenes.heading: INT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - AN HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: At Kennedy Space Center, President Orlean asks if the mission can still work with six drones down, while Isherwell claims 24 drones are enough but won't answer how many more they can lose.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY'S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: Randall's sons and Teddy watch news coverage while dinner cooks at the Mindy home.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: The BEAD drones explode in a giant flash as the explosive separation stage commences in space.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: The crowd at Kennedy Space Center stares at the sky and the asteroid, which is now twice the size of the moon.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY'S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN
scenes.summary: The Mindys, Kate, Quentin and Teddy sit down to a beautiful dinner with turkey, salmon and all the fixings.

scenes.heading: INT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER CONTROL CENTER - AN HOUR LATER
scenes.summary: At Kennedy Space Center, the Flight Director reports four misfires and that the asteroid is still whole, while Isherwell and President Orlean both leave the room.
scenes.turningPointNote: The mission has failed - the asteroid remains intact and on collision course

scenes.heading: INT. MINDY’S DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - 20 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: Kate observes the asteroid is still in the sky, but Randall insists they stop talking about it and enjoy their meal together.

scenes.heading: EXT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: News anchors report the BEAD drones failed to break apart the asteroid as two helicopters leave Kennedy Space Center and the crowd begins to panic.

scenes.heading: INT. MINDY'S DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The dinner group joins hands for grace, with Randall expressing gratitude for his family, Oglethorpe for knowing them, and Kate saying at least they tried.

scenes.heading: INT. COMMAND CENTER - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The Flight Director asks Jason where the President is while a Command Tech abandons his post to see his husband and others start fleeing.

scenes.heading: INT. THE MINDY'S HOME - LANSING MICHIGAN - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: Teddy speaks on the phone with someone he loves while New York City descends into chaos with traffic, fires, and violence.

scenes.heading: INT. PRESIDENTIAL HELICOPTER - SAME TIME
scenes.summary: President Orlean makes a satellite phone call from her helicopter.

scenes.heading: INT. MINDY'S DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: President Orlean calls Randall's landline to tell him about an escape ship with cryo-chambers for 200 people designed to find an earth-like planet.
scenes.turningPointNote: Reveals the elite have a secret escape plan while abandoning everyone else

scenes.heading: INT. KENNEDY SPACE CENTER CONTROL CENTER - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The Flight Director abandons his post to see his daughter, leaving Jason alone in the nearly empty control center.

scenes.heading: EXT. PLANET EARTH - 30 MINUTES LATER
scenes.summary: The asteroid begins entering Earth's atmosphere at incredible velocity, creating violent ripples and bright flares from friction.
scenes.turningPointNote: The asteroid impact begins - the point of no return for humanity

scenes.heading: INT. MINDY'S DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The family enjoys dessert while the house shudders, discussing store-bought apple pie and childhood memories.

scenes.heading: EXT. OPEN SKY - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The asteroid rips through the sky like fabric being torn.

scenes.heading: INT. MINDY'S DINING ROOM - MICHIGAN - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: As the house shakes violently and they discuss coffee, the asteroid impacts Earth, splitting the planet apart in a massive explosion of debris.
scenes.turningPointNote: The asteroid destroys Earth and kills all life on the planet

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - 2 YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: Two years later, the escape ship navigates past an exploding galaxy toward an array of stars.

scenes.heading: EXT. SPACE - 5 YEARS LATER
scenes.summary: Five years later, the dented and dirty ship orbits a blue-green planet.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW PLANET - DAY
scenes.summary: The ship lands in a clearing of blue grass surrounded by oddly shaped trees as deer-bird creatures leap past and the door opens.

scenes.heading: INT. SPACE SHIP - CONTINUOUS
scenes.summary: The cryo-chambers containing CEOs, lobbyists, President Orlean, and other elites calculate and finally open.

scenes.heading: EXT. NEW PLANET - MOMENTS LATER
scenes.summary: The naked, elderly elites emerge from the ship as Isherwell reports the oxygen levels and 78 percent cryo-chamber success rate while another CEO samples alien fruit.

structuralWarnings.text: All 3 key moments were auto-corrected by Phase 1C
